title: Delivery and ordering on a channel
description: Realtime transports do not guarantee delivery across disconnects; ordering and at-least-once need application support.
body: Use when correctness depends on receiving every update in order. A WebSocket/SSE stream delivers in order while connected, but a disconnect drops anything sent during the gap, and naive reconnect does not backfill. Do not treat 'message sent' as 'message received and applied'. Actions: add sequence ids and client cursors so reconnect replays the gap, make apply idempotent for duplicates, and for critical data require client acks or a periodic state reconciliation. Validation signals include a disconnect test that recovers all missed messages in order. Risk boundary: assuming guaranteed in-order delivery over a raw socket leads to permanent client/server state divergence.
